title: Quiz: Training Quantum Neural Networks
description: Test your understanding of QNN training techniques including gradient computation, shot noise, and optimization

questions.question: What is the shift value used in the standard parameter shift rule for Pauli rotation gates?
questions.explanation: The standard parameter shift rule uses a shift of pi/2 for Pauli rotation gates (RX, RY, RZ). The gradient is computed as 0.5 * [f(theta + pi/2) - f(theta - pi/2)].

questions.question: How many circuit evaluations are needed to compute the gradient of n parameters using the parameter shift rule?
questions.explanation: The parameter shift rule requires 2 circuit evaluations per parameter (one with +shift and one with -shift), totaling 2n evaluations for n parameters.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of SPSA over standard gradient descent for QNN training?

options.text: Only 2 circuit evaluations regardless of parameter count

questions.explanation: SPSA (Simultaneous Perturbation Stochastic Approximation) requires only 2 circuit evaluations per iteration, regardless of the number of parameters, making it very efficient for large parameter counts.

questions.question: The quantum natural gradient uses the Fubini-Study metric tensor to account for the geometry of the parameter space.
questions.explanation: The quantum natural gradient multiplies the standard gradient by the inverse of the Fubini-Study metric tensor (quantum Fisher information), which captures the geometry of the quantum state manifold.

questions.question: How does shot noise in gradient estimation scale with the number of measurement shots N?
questions.explanation: Shot noise follows standard statistical scaling where the standard deviation of the estimate decreases as 1/sqrt(N). To halve the error, you need 4 times as many shots.

questions.question: What is the main disadvantage of finite difference gradients compared to parameter shift?

options.text: Only works for specific gate types

questions.explanation: Finite differences introduce truncation error from the approximation f'(x) = [f(x+e) - f(x-e)]/(2e). This error depends on epsilon and is not present in parameter shift which gives exact gradients.

questions.question: Which optimizer is generally recommended as a robust default choice for training QNNs with noisy gradients?
questions.explanation: Adam is typically the best default choice for QNN training because it combines momentum and adaptive learning rates, making it robust to the noisy gradients typical of quantum measurements.

questions.question: Which are valid strategies for handling shot noise in QNN training? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Increase the number of measurement shots
Use optimizers robust to noise like Adam

questions.explanation: Increasing shots (a), averaging gradients (b), and using robust optimizers (c) all help handle shot noise. Smaller batch sizes (d) would actually increase gradient variance, not reduce it.
